Flammable gases, category 1

Oxidizing gases, category 1
Oxidizing liquids, categories 1, 2, 3

GHS04: Compressed Gas.copy:
Compressed gases
Liquefied gases
Refrigerated liquefied gases
Dissolved gases
e.g. liquid nitrogen, liquid oxygen, liquid helium
GHS04: Compressed Gas.title: GHS04: Compressed Gas

GHS06: Toxic.copy:
Acute toxicity (oral, dermal, inhalation), categories 1, 2, 3
e.g. manganese heptoxide (fire diamond rating at health hazard is 4)
GHS06: Toxic.title: GHS06: Toxic

GHS07: Health Hazard/Hazardous to Ozone.copy:
Acute toxicity (oral, dermal, inhalation), category 4
Skin irritation, categories 2, 3
Eye irritation, category 2A
Skin sensitization, category 1
Specific target organ toxicity following single exposure, category 3
Respiratory tract irritation
Narcotic effects
GHS07: Health Hazard/Hazardous to Ozone.title: GHS07: Health Hazard/Hazardous to Ozone Layer

GHS08: Serious Health hazard.copy:
Respiratory sensitization, category 1
Germ cell mutagenicity, categories 1A, 1B, 2
Carcinogenicity, categories 1A, 1B, 2
Reproductive toxicity, categories 1A, 1B, 2
Specific target organ toxicity following single exposure, categories 1, 2
Specific target organ toxicity following repeated exposure, categories 1, 2
Aspiration hazard, categories 1, 2
e.g. chromium, cadmium
GHS08: Serious Health hazard.title: GHS08: Serious Health hazard

GHS05: Corrosive.copy:
Explosives, divisions 1.5, 1.6
Flammable gases, category 2
Self-reactive substances and mixtures, type G  (see HAZMAT Class 4 Flammable solids)
Organic peroxides, type G
Skin corrosion, categories 1A, 1B, 1C
Serious eye damage, category 1
GHS05: Corrosive.title: GHS05: Corrosive

GHS09: Hazardous to the Environment.copy:
Acute hazards to the aquatic environment, category 1
Chronic hazards to the aquatic environment, categories 1, 2
GHS09: Hazardous to the Environment.title: GHS09: Hazardous to the Environment

Divisions 1.1–1.3.copy: Note: The asterisks are replaced by the class number and compatibility code
Divisions 1.1–1.3.title: Divisions 1.1–1.3

Division 1.4.copy: Explosives – Substances and articles which are classified as explosives but which present no significant hazard
Division 1.4.title: Division 1.4

Division 1.5.copy: Explosives – Very insensitive substances which have a mass explosion hazard
Division 1.5.title: Division 1.5

Division 2.1.copy: Flammable gases – Gases which at 20 °C and a standard pressure of 101.3 kPa:
Division 2.1.title: Division 2.1

Class 3.copy: Flammable liquids – Liquids which have a flash point of less than 60 °C and which are capable of sustaining combustion
Class 3.title: Class 3

Division 5.1.copy: Oxidizing substances – Substances which, while in themselves not necessarily combustible, may, generally by yielding oxygen, cause, or contribute to, the combustion of other material
Division 5.1.title: Division 5.1

Division 5.2.copy: Organic peroxides – Organic substances which contain the bivalent –O–O– structure and may be considered derivatives of hydrogen peroxide, where one or both of the hydrogen atoms have been replaced by organic radicals
Division 5.2.title: Division 5.2

Division 6.1.copy: Toxic substances – Substances with an LD50 value ≤ 300 mg/kg (oral) or ≤ 1000 mg/kg (dermal) or an LC50 value ≤ 4000 ml/m3 (inhalation of dusts or mists)
Division 6.1.title: Division 6.1
